name: Set Up and Use Private npm Registries
description: Configure and publish to private npm registries using Verdaccio, GitHub Packages, or npm Teams for internal package distribution.

steps.title: Run a local Verdaccio registry
steps.description: Start a self-hosted npm registry for development and testing.
steps.tip: Verdaccio proxies the public npm registry by default, so you can install public packages through it while hosting private ones.

steps.title: Configure npm to use the private registry
steps.description: Set the registry URL for a specific scope.
steps.tip: Scope-based registry config lets you use a private registry for @myorg/* packages while everything else goes to the public registry.

steps.title: Authenticate with the private registry
steps.description: Login to the private registry to publish and install private packages.

steps.title: Publish to the private registry
steps.description: Push a package to your private registry.

steps.title: Configure .npmrc for the project
steps.description: Create a project-level .npmrc to share registry config with the team.
steps.warning: Never commit auth tokens to .npmrc. Use environment variables and add .npmrc to .gitignore if it contains secrets. Use a .npmrc template checked into git with token placeholders.

steps.title: Configure GitHub Packages as an alternative
steps.description: Use GitHub Packages as a private registry for your organization.
steps.command: npm config set @myorg:registry https://npm.pkg.github.com && npm login --registry https://npm.pkg.github.com --scope=@myorg

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Private npm Registry Setup with Verdaccio
set -euo pipefail

REGISTRY_PORT=${1:-4873}
SCOPE=${2:-"@myorg"}

echo "=== Starting Verdaccio ==="
docker run -d \
  --name verdaccio \
  -p "$REGISTRY_PORT:4873" \
  -v verdaccio-storage:/verdaccio/storage \
  -v verdaccio-conf:/verdaccio/conf \
  verdaccio/verdaccio

echo "Waiting for Verdaccio to start..."
sleep 3

REGISTRY_URL="http://localhost:$REGISTRY_PORT"

echo ""
echo "=== Configuring npm ==="
npm config set "$SCOPE:registry" "$REGISTRY_URL/"

echo ""
echo "=== Registry Status ==="
curl -s "$REGISTRY_URL/-/ping" && echo " Registry is healthy" || echo "Registry is not responding"

echo ""
echo "=== Project .npmrc Template ==="
cat << NPMRC
# Add to .npmrc (do NOT commit tokens)
$SCOPE:registry=$REGISTRY_URL/
//localhost:$REGISTRY_PORT/:_authToken=\${NPM_TOKEN}
NPMRC

echo ""
echo "=== Next Steps ==="
echo "1. Login:   npm login --registry $REGISTRY_URL"
echo "2. Publish: npm publish --registry $REGISTRY_URL"
echo "3. Install: npm install $SCOPE/mypackage"
echo "4. Web UI:  open $REGISTRY_URL"

faq.question: What are the options for hosting a private npm registry?
faq.answer: Options include: Verdaccio (self-hosted, free, Docker-friendly), GitHub Packages (free for public, paid for private), npm Teams/Pro (paid, npmjs.com), GitLab Packages (built into GitLab), and Artifactory/Nexus for enterprise setups.

faq.question: How do I configure different registries for different scopes?
faq.answer: Set scope-specific registries in .npmrc: '@myorg:registry=https://private.example.com'. Unscoped packages will continue to use the default npm registry. You can have multiple scopes pointing to different registries.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication tokens safely?
faq.answer: Use environment variables in .npmrc: '//registry.example.com/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}'. Set the NPM_TOKEN env var in your shell profile or CI secrets. Never commit actual tokens to version control.
